title: Shut Off a Local Water Valve
summary: Locate and turn off a local water valve in case of a leak.
learnerPromise: You will be able to locate and turn off a local water valve to stop water flow to a sink, toilet, or appliance during a leak.
whyItMatters: Knowing how to shut off water quickly can prevent serious water damage during a leak or burst pipe. It is one of the most important emergency skills for any home.
realLifeUses: Stopping a toilet that is overflowing
Responding to a leaking sink pipe under a cabinet
Turning off water before replacing a faucet or appliance
Reacting to a burst pipe or major leak until help arrives
materialsNeeded: Flashlight (if the valve is in a dark area)
result: Water flow stopped at the local valve, confirmed by testing the fixture.
youWillLearn: How to identify different types of water valves
How to turn a valve clockwise to shut off water
How to confirm water is off by testing the fixture
How to locate the main water shut-off for the whole home
miniChallenge: Find and identify the water shut-off valve under one sink or behind one toilet in your home. Turn it off and back on.
steps: Locate the valve near the fixture — usually under sinks or behind toilets on the water supply line.
Identify the valve type: round wheel handle (gate valve) or flat lever (ball valve).
Turn the valve clockwise (to the right) until it is snug for a wheel handle, or turn the lever 90 degrees so it is perpendicular to the pipe.
Open the faucet or flush the toilet to confirm water flow has stopped.
When ready, turn the valve counterclockwise (to the left) or return the lever parallel to the pipe to restore water.
completionCriteria: Water flow to the fixture is confirmed stopped after closing the valve
Water flow is restored smoothly after reopening the valve
commonProblems: Valve is stuck and will not turn — use an adjustable wrench carefully, or ask a plumber
Not sure if it is fully closed — test by running the faucet; if water stops, it is closed
Valve starts dripping when turned — it may be old; open and close it gently a few times
tips: Test local valves once a year to keep them from seizing shut
Know where the main water shut-off is in case local valves fail
If a pipe is actively spraying, shut off the main valve and call a professional immediately
helpBoundary: If water is spraying, flooding, or the main valve will not turn, shut off what you can and call a plumber or emergency maintenance immediately. Do not attempt to repair pressurized pipes on your own.
